name: ExtensionActorDefinition_Publisher
title: R5: Name of the publisher/steward (organization or individual) (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `ActorDefinition.publisher` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`ActorDefinition.publisher` 0..1 `string`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
* R5 `ActorDefinition.publisher` 0..1 `string`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `ActorDefinition.publisher` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ActorDefinition` is not mapped.
Element `ActorDefinition.publisher` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Name of the publisher/steward (organization or individual) (new)
element.comment:
Element `ActorDefinition.publisher` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ActorDefinition` is not mapped.
Element `ActorDefinition.publisher` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
Usually an organization but may be an individual. The publisher (or steward) of the actor definition is the organization or individual primarily responsible for the maintenance and upkeep of the actor definition. This is not necessarily the same individual or organization that developed and initially authored the content. The publisher is the primary point of contact for questions or issues with the actor definition. This item SHOULD be populated unless the information is available from context.

element.short: Name of the publisher/steward (organization or individual)
element.definition: The name of the organization or individual responsible for the release and ongoing maintenance of the actor definition.
element.comment: Usually an organization but may be an individual. The publisher (or steward) of the actor definition is the organization or individual primarily responsible for the maintenance and upkeep of the actor definition. This is not necessarily the same individual or organization that developed and initially authored the content.
